text: A database is a system for storing and organizing data. Most traditional databases use tables, which are like spreadsheets with rows and columns. To manage this data, we use SQL (Structured Query Language), which lets us run queries to find, add, or update information. However, not all data fits neatly into tables. That is where NoSQL databases come in—they handle unstructured data like documents or social media posts. A key part of building a database is data modeling, which means planning how your data will be structured. To speed up searches, we also use indexes, which work like a book’s index to help find data quickly. Understanding these concepts helps you choose the right database for your needs.
